description_long: This object is a vintage IBM personal computer setup, featuring a CRT monitor with a prominent IBM logo displayed on the screen, accompanied by a matching keyboard and a rectangular system unit beneath the monitor. The monitor has a thick, boxy frame with a slightly curved glass display, and the screen bezel is wide and flat, with a subtle color contrast between the bezel and the display area. The system unit below the monitor has multiple ventilation slots and two large drive bays, likely for floppy disks, as well as a visible power cord extending from the rear. The keyboard is full-sized, with a classic layout and raised keys, connected to the system unit by a cable. The entire assembly is constructed from light gray plastic, with a utilitarian, industrial design typical of early personal computers. No visible buttons or ports are present on the front or sides of the monitor, and the rear is plain except for a small protruding panel and the power cord.
description: A vintage IBM CRT computer monitor with a thick bezel, paired with a matching keyboard and system unit in light gray plastic. The monitor displays the IBM logo and features a classic, boxy design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the IBM logo on the screen, thick bezel, drive bays, ventilation slots, and the full keyboard layout.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the CRT monitor, the flat rectangular system unit, and the keyboard positioned to the left.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the plain back of the monitor with a small protruding panel and the power cord, no visible ports or buttons.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the monitor's sloped back, the system unit's length, and the keyboard's angled position.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The screen bezel is thick, flat, and made of light gray plastic with squared edges and a subtle color contrast to the display area. It has a utilitarian, industrial appearance with no decorative elements.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a flat, rectangular system unit that acts as a base, with no visible adjustability or separate pedestal; the base is solid and matches the monitor's material.
attribute_button_placement: A single power button is implied on the lower right of the system unit's front panel, with no visible buttons on the monitor itself.
attribute_brand_logo: A large IBM logo is prominently displayed on the screen, centered and occupying most of the display area; no physical logo is visible on the bezel or casing.
